title: ToolVersion
description: Per-version installation and support data for a tool. Lives at `tools/<tool_id>/versions/<version>.json`. The schema captures three intentionally separate concerns: (1) `support_matrix` — what we *claim* runs; (2) `tested[]` — what CI has *evidenced*; (3) `install_methods[]` — *how* to install. The claim/evidence split is deliberate per the catalog design memo and must not be conflated.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version for this version document. Bumped on breaking shape changes; consumers reject unknown versions.

tool.description: Tool id this version belongs to. Must match the parent directory name (`tools/<tool>/versions/`) and the `id` field of the corresponding tool index.

version.description: Version string. Must match the file basename (`<version>.json`). Free-form so partial versions like Python `3.12` are allowed.

released.description: Upstream release date (calendar date in the project's timezone — exact-time precision not required).

channel.description: Release channel id this version belongs to. Must reference a channel declared on the parent tool's `channels` map; cross-ref enforcement is the consumer's job.

support_matrix.description: CLAIM: which OS/version/arch combinations we *say* this tool version runs on. Authoritative for stibbons recommendation and luggage `resolve` output. Distinct from `tested[]`, which is observational evidence.

tested.description: EVIDENCE: CI run records that demonstrate this version was actually exercised on a given OS/version/arch. Each entry pins to a CI run URL and a result so claims can be audited. May be empty during initial catalog population.

requires.description: Version-level compatibility expectations that must hold across ALL install methods (e.g., `cargo_X needs rust >=1.8.5`, `python_3.13 needs openssl >=3`). Solver input — captures the diamond case where two install methods of two different tools both pull in the same dep at conflicting constraints. Distinct from `install_methods[].dependencies[]`, which is the physical install chain for one specific method on one specific platform.

install_methods.description: Ordered list of install strategies. luggage walks them in array order, picking the first whose `platform` predicate matches the target host. At least one method is required.

uninstall.description: Optional inverse of install — commands luggage runs to remove this tool version. Best-effort; some tools have no clean uninstall.

commands.description: Shell command strings executed in order. Each runs in a clean shell with the tool's environment exported.

metadata.description: Bookkeeping. `added_at` records when this entry was first written to the catalog; `updated_at` is bumped by every scanner-driven edit.

added_at.description: When this version entry was first added to the catalog.

updated_at.description: When this version entry was last modified by a scanner or hand-edit.

schema_version.description: Echo of the document `schemaVersion` so changelog tooling can detect schema drift on a single field.

Distro.description: OS / distribution identifier. Free-form string so non-Linux targets can be added later (e.g., `darwin`, `windows`). Common Linux values: `debian`, `ubuntu`, `alpine`, `rhel`, `fedora`, `arch`.

Arch.description: CPU architecture. Common values: `amd64`, `arm64`, `armv7`, `riscv64`. Other values allowed for forward compatibility.

SupportEntry.description: One row of the support matrix — a (distro, distro version, arch) triple plus a status. The remediation fields (`reason`, `tracking_url`, `recheck_at`) are intended for `status: unsupported` rows so the resolver can surface why a combination was refused and when to revisit; populating them on `supported` or `untested` rows is allowed but unused by current consumers.

os_version.description: Distro version (e.g., `12` for Debian Bookworm, `3.21` for Alpine, `9` for RHEL). Optional — when omitted, the row applies to all versions of `os`.

status.description: Claim about this combination: `supported` (we will install and expect it to work), `unsupported` (we refuse), `untested` (we don't know).

notes.description: Free-form reviewer notes (e.g., known caveats, required system packages).

reason.description: Structured explanation for `status: unsupported`. Surfaced verbatim by the resolver's remediation menu (e.g., `upstream dropped armv7 in 1.95`, `glibc too old`).

tracking_url.description: Link to an upstream issue, advisory, or PR documenting the unsupported state. Populated when there is something for a user to follow.

recheck_at.description: Calendar date after which the catalog scanner should re-evaluate this row. Useful for time-bound unsupported reasons (e.g., `EOL distro version reached`).

TestEntry.description: One CI run providing evidence that a given (distro, distro version, arch) actually worked.

os_version.description: Distro version under test. Optional but strongly recommended for evidence rows.

ci_run.description: URL of the CI run that produced this evidence (e.g., GitHub Actions run URL).

result.description: Outcome of the run for this combination.

notes.description: Free-form notes (e.g., flake reason for `fail`, why `skip`).

Dependency.description: A typed reference to another catalog tool (typically `kind: system_package`) that must be present in the resolver's view of the install plan. Shared shape used by `requires[]` (version-level compatibility) and `install_methods[].dependencies[]` (method-level install chain). The `version` and `version_constraint` fields are mutually exclusive: `version` is an exact pin (forbidden when `tool` targets a `kind: system_package` entry — the host package manager picks, the catalog only guards via `version_constraint`); `version_constraint` is a comparator string parsed per the target's `version_style`. The system_package-pin rule cannot be enforced in pure JSON Schema across files and is checked by the cross-ref validator script.

oneOf.description: Neither a pin nor a constraint — `take whatever the resolver picks`.

tool.description: Target tool's `id`. Must exist in the catalog (cross-ref validator enforces).

version.description: Exact version pin. Forbidden when the target tool's `kind` is `system_package` (the host package manager owns version selection — the catalog only guards via `version_constraint`). Enforced by the cross-ref validator script, not by JSON Schema, because `kind` lookup crosses files.

version_constraint.description: Comparator expression (e.g., `>=1.8.5`, `~=2.4`). Parsed per the target tool's `version_style`. The placeholder comparator currently shipped only accepts the literal `*`; real comparator wiring lands in a follow-up issue.

purpose.description: When this dep is needed. `build` = present at install time only (compiler, header files); `runtime` = needed every time the tool runs; `both` (default) = needed throughout.

required.description: Whether the resolver must refuse to install when the dep is unmet (`true`, default) or merely warn (`false`). Soft deps surface in stibbons output but do not block.

platforms.description: Optional narrowing — restrict this Dependency to the listed distros. When a Dependency targets a `kind: system_package`, every listed platform must appear in the target's `system_package.platforms` map; the cross-ref validator enforces this.

InstallMethod.description: One way to install this version. luggage selects the first method whose `platform` matches and whose `verification` tier is acceptable to the active policy.

name.description: Method identifier (e.g., `rustup`, `apt`, `tarball`, `script`). Used in luggage logs and error messages.

platform.description: Predicate restricting which hosts this method applies to. Omit to mean any platform. luggage AND-combines all listed fields. String values match exactly; arrays match if the host's value is in the array.

os.description: Distro id or array of ids this method targets.

os_version.description: Distro version or array of versions this method targets. Compared as strings.

arch.description: CPU architecture or array of architectures this method targets.

verification.description: Download-verification configuration. Carries the 4-tier model from `lib/base/checksum-verification.sh` (Tier 1 cryptographic signature, Tier 2 pinned checksum baked into this file, Tier 3 publisher-served checksum, Tier 4 calculated TOFU). The per-tier conditional rules in `allOf` enforce that each tier carries exactly the fields that tier needs and no fields belonging to other tiers — see `docs/verification.md` for the field-mapping table.

tier.description: Verification tier: 1 = cryptographic signature (GPG/sigstore), 2 = pinned checksum baked into this file, 3 = checksum fetched from the publisher at install time, 4 = calculated TOFU (no verification, security warning required).

algorithm.description: Verification algorithm. Tier 1 uses `gpg` or `sigstore`; Tiers 2-3 use `sha256` or `sha512`; Tier 4 (when present) records the algorithm of the TOFU digest, typically `sha256`.

pinned_checksum.description: Tier 2: hex-encoded checksum baked into this version file. Audited via git review.

checksum_url_template.description: Tier 3: URL template the publisher serves the checksum at. Accepts the same placeholder vocabulary as `source_url_template` — see that field's description for the full reserved list and resolution rules.

gpg_key_url.description: Tier 1 (GPG): URL of the publisher's public key.

signature_url_template.description: Tier 1 (GPG): URL template for the detached signature corresponding to the downloaded artifact.

tofu.description: Tier 4: explicit acknowledgment that no upstream verification exists. Must be `true` on Tier 4 entries; consumers must surface a security warning when this is set.

then.description: Tier 1: cryptographic signature. Requires `algorithm` ∈ {gpg, sigstore} plus the matching key/identity fields. Forbids tier 2/3/4 carriers.
Tier 2: pinned checksum baked into this file. Requires `algorithm` ∈ {sha256, sha512} and `pinned_checksum`. Forbids tier 1/3/4 carriers.
Tier 3: publisher-served checksum. Requires `algorithm` ∈ {sha256, sha512} and `checksum_url_template`. Forbids tier 1/2/4 carriers.
Tier 4: calculated TOFU. Requires `tofu: true` (explicit acknowledgment). `algorithm`, when present, must be a hash function. Forbids tier 1/2/3 carriers.

source_url_template.description:
URL template for the artifact to download. Reserved placeholder vocabulary, all resolved by the consumer (luggage) at install time:

- `{version}` — the version string from the parent file (`<v>.json` basename).
- `{arch}` — host CPU arch normalized to amd64/arm64/armv7/riscv64.
- `{os}` — distro id (`debian`, `ubuntu`, `alpine`, `rhel`, `fedora`, ...).
- `{os_version}` — distro version string.
- `{libc}` — `gnu` or `musl`, derived from the distro.
- `{rustup_target}` — full rust target triple (e.g., `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`).
- `{distro_family}` — family rollup: `debian` (covers debian+ubuntu), `rhel` (covers rhel+fedora+rocky), `alpine`.

Resolution rules:

1. Resolution is the consumer's job (luggage); this schema only documents what tool authors may rely on.
2. Unknown placeholders are passed through verbatim by ajv (the schema does not enforce the vocabulary as a regex). Consumers MUST reject unknown placeholders rather than substituting empty strings — that is luggage's responsibility, not ajv's.
3. Placeholder names are reserved. Adding a new placeholder is a non-breaking schema change (existing files remain valid); renaming an existing one bumps `schemaVersion`.

invoke.description: How to run an installer artifact (e.g., rustup-init script). Omit for plain tarballs that luggage extracts directly.

args.description: Argument vector passed to the installer.

env.description: Environment variables exported for the installer process.

post_install.description: Steps run after the primary install succeeds (e.g., `rustup component add`, capability tweaks). Each entry is a structured step rather than a raw shell line so luggage can sandbox / audit them.

kind.description: Step type. `command` runs a single binary with args; `cargo_install` is shorthand for cargo subcommand installs; `component_add` is shorthand for `rustup component add`.

version.description: For `kind: cargo_install` — crate version (must be exact per the cargo-install policy in CLAUDE.md).

dependencies.description: Method-level install chain: physical packages and tools that must be installed BEFORE this install method runs. Each entry references another catalog tool (typically `kind: system_package`) by id. Distinct from the version-level `requires[]`, which expresses compatibility expectations across all install methods rather than what to fetch for THIS one. Replaces the pre-#6 `system_packages: { distro: [strings] }` shape, which is gone — files carrying `system_packages` are rejected by `additionalProperties: false`.
